steps.title: Define the reference
steps.content: The friend says his soul is calm because of the lack of "anvil and strife." Those words suggest noise, hard labor, pressure, and conflict. Since the friend is peaceful specifically because those things are absent, the reference helps define his present tranquility by naming its opposite.

steps.title: Compare the choices
steps.content: Choice A wrongly shifts the contrast to nature versus human feelings. Choice B focuses on unspoken understanding, but the friend does speak about his calm in this line. Choice D reverses the meaning by treating work and social engagement as values the friend wants, even though their absence is what calms him. Choice C matches the function of the phrase: it contrasts with the friend's current peace. The answer is C.
